name: KyberSwap Elastic Tick-Crossing Drain
tldr: KyberSwap Elastic lost ~$48M across multiple chains when an attacker exploited a precision/tick-crossing flaw in the concentrated-liquidity swap math; the largest single-chain drain was on Ethereum, with parallel drains on Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon, Base, Avalanche, and BSC pools.
coreContradiction: Because computeSwapStep rounded the pre-tick amountIn using base+reinvestment liquidity but the post-swap price check used only base liquidity, the pool could cross a tick without ever calling _updateLiquidityAndCrossTick, letting the same liquidity be counted on both sides of the boundary.

attackSteps.label: Flash loan WETH
attackSteps.detail: Attacker EOA 0x5027...be836 deployed exploit contract 0xaF2A...CDC13 and funded it with ~2,000 WETH flash-loaned from AAVE on Ethereum.

attackSteps.label: Price push past LP range
attackSteps.detail: Swap ~6.85 WETH into the frxETH/WETH KyberSwap Elastic pool (0xFd7B...4703) to push sqrtPrice above every existing LP position's upper tick, leaving the pool with zero active base liquidity.

attackSteps.label: Mint and partially burn
attackSteps.detail: Attacker minted a tiny position in the narrow range [tick 110909, 111310] and burned most of it so that only their liquidity was active inside that range while the reinvestment curve still held residual liquidity.

attackSteps.label: Trigger double-count tick cross
attackSteps.detail: A precisely-sized reverse swap made computeSwapStep overestimate amountIn (it folded in reinvestment liquidity) so post-swap sqrtPrice exceeded the boundary tick without satisfying the strict inequality that calls _updateLiquidityAndCrossTick; liquidity was effectively counted twice.

attackSteps.label: Drain pool and repeat
attackSteps.detail: The flawed accounting let the attacker pull ~396 WETH out for ~0.006 frxETH, ~9 WETH more than the forward leg; the exploit contract repeated the pattern across multiple Ethereum Elastic pools and replayed it on six other chains for a total ~$48M loss.

fundFlow.note: net drain from frxETH/WETH pool via reverse swap; contract repeated pattern on other Elastic pools
proceeds forwarded to KyberSwap Exploiter 1 EOA after flash-loan repayment
